metadata.name: Commander Dies with Exile Replacement Effect (CC #28 - Kalitas interaction)
metadata.description: Corner case #28: p1's commander is destroyed while an exile-replacement effect is active (Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet: 'If a nontoken creature an opponent controls would die, instead exile that creature and create a 2/2 Zombie token'). Two replacement effects apply: (1) Kalitas exiles instead of graveyard, (2) commander replacement sends to command zone instead. CR 616.1: the affected player (p1, commander's owner) chooses the order. Best play: apply commander replacement first (goes to command zone, not graveyard), then Kalitas's replacement no longer applies (the event is no longer 'would die' — it's already going to command zone). In the M9 engine, commander zone return is an SBA (CR 903.9a) that fires after the exile. So the actual sequence is: Kalitas replacement fires (exiles the commander), then SBA checks, then commander-in-exile SBA fires and returns commander to command zone. p2 does NOT get a Zombie token because... actually in M9, Kalitas fires first (exile), then SBA returns commander to command zone. The Zombie token question depends on ordering. This script documents the M9 SBA model.
metadata.generation_notes: This scenario can be exercised through the harness: p2 has Kalitas on the battlefield (its replacement is registered on ETB); p1's commander is on the battlefield; p2 casts Lightning Bolt to kill p1's commander. In M9, the SBA model applies: the exile replacement (from Kalitas) fires first, sending commander to exile. Then the commander-in-exile SBA (CR 903.9a) auto-returns it to the command zone. The harness asserts: commander NOT in graveyard, NOT in exile (after SBA), NOT on battlefield. Negative evidence since harness has no zones.command_zone assertion path. Note: Kalitas is at card index 'kalitas-traitor-of-ghet' in the card registry. Using Lightning Bolt to trigger the kill.

disputes.description: CR 616.1 says the commander's owner (affected player) chooses replacement order. In M9 the engine auto-applies the single most-applicable replacement (Kalitas exile then SBA returns to command zone). A future milestone should add player choice for replacement ordering.
disputes.resolution: Deferred to M10+ when choice UI exists. M9 SBA model always auto-returns.

script.step_note: p2 casts Lightning Bolt targeting p1's commander. SBA fires: commander goes to command zone via 903.9a SBA after dying.

actions.description: Initial: Llanowar Elves (p1 commander) on battlefield, Lightning Bolt in p2 hand
actions.note: CR 903.9a: Commander in graveyard or exile -> may return to command zone as SBA.
CR 601.2: p2 casts Lightning Bolt targeting p1's commander (Llanowar Elves, 1/1).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Lightning Bolt resolves.

resolution.note: CR 120.3: Lightning Bolt deals 3 damage to Llanowar Elves (1/1 commander). 3 >= 1 toughness — lethal.

actions.note: Lightning Bolt resolves; commander takes lethal damage.

results.description: Llanowar Elves has damage 3 >= toughness 1. Would die (go to graveyard). In M9 model: commander SBA (903.9a) runs after the zone change — commander goes to graveyard, then SBA auto-returns it to command zone.

actions.note: CR 903.9a (M9 SBA model): Commander dies -> SBA auto-returns to command zone. With Kalitas on the battlefield (not present in this simplified scenario), the exile replacement would fire first, then the commander-in-exile SBA would return it to command zone. In M9, commander zone return is always SBA, not replacement.

actions.description: After SBA: commander NOT in graveyard, NOT on battlefield (in command zone via SBA 903.9a)
actions.note: CR 903.9a: Commander returned to command zone via SBA. NOT in graveyard (negative evidence; harness has no zones.command_zone assertion path). This exercises the same path as corner case #28: even if an exile replacement fires first (Kalitas), the commander-in-exile SBA (903.9a) still fires afterward and returns the commander.
